title: matches_pattern double-checks .len() manually instead of using strip_suffix
description:
LOCATION: src/watch.rs:130-133

FINDING: The glob match for *.ext patterns manually checks file_name.len() > ext.len() + 1 and file_name.as_bytes()[file_name.len() - ext.len() - 1] == b'.', which reproduces what file_name.strip_suffix(&format\!(".{ext}")).is_some() does in one call. The byte-index arithmetic is correct but requires a re-read to verify it handles the dot correctly and doesn't panic (it doesn't, because the length check guards it).

IMPACT: The intent (does filename end with .{ext}) is not obvious from the implementation. A reader must mentally execute the arithmetic. If the logic is ever changed (e.g., to support multi-component extensions like .tar.gz), the manual byte indexing is a footgun.
